title: SystemVerilog interface fields are implicitly defined?
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

```
cd tests/svinterfaces
yosys> read_verilog -sv svinterface1.sv
1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: svinterface1.sv
Parsing SystemVerilog input from `svinterface1.sv' to AST representation.
Generating RTLIL representation for module `\TopModule'.
svinterface1.sv:22: Warning: Identifier `\MyInterfaceInstance.mysig_out' is implicitly declared.
svinterface1.sv:25: Warning: Identifier `\MyInterfaceInstance.setting' is implicitly declared.
svinterface1.sv:27: Warning: Identifier `\MyInterfaceInstance.passThrough' is implicitly declared.
[skip]
```

This gets turned into an error with added `default_nettype none`.



## Expected behavior

No warning/error with `default_nettype none`

## Actual behavior

Warning/error.

Are there any reasons not to have default_nettype enabled in all the tests? As far as I understand setting it to none is a way to catch accidental typos and it's used all over the place...

yosys> read_verilog -sv svinterface1.sv
